goal: Create an 'oiled_steel' PhysicsMaterial with mu_s=0.15 / mu_d=0.10, bind via MaterialAPI, set frictionCombineMode explicitly, validate with a grasp-and-lift test.
thoughts: PhysX defaults to frictionCombineMode=AVERAGE or MIN — must set it explicitly (MULTIPLY is common for lubricated contacts but not default) or the effective friction is a surprise. Stribeck / speed-dependent friction is NOT modeled by PhysX — sim will be drier than real oiled steel. Don't crank to mu=1.0 to hide slip — that masks real design problems (gripper force margin, part geometry). Validation: scripted grasp-and-lift, ablate friction, confirm slip threshold.
code:
# 1. Create a named oiled_steel PhysicsMaterial
create_material(
    prim_path="/World/Materials/OiledSteel",
    material_type="physics",
    attributes={
        "physics:staticFriction":   0.15,
        "physics:dynamicFriction":  0.10,
        "physics:restitution":      0.1,
        "physics:density":          "<STEEL_DENSITY>"
    }
)

# 2. Explicitly set frictionCombineMode (default is a surprise)
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/Materials/OiledSteel",
    attribute="physxMaterial:frictionCombineMode",
    value="min"   # or 'multiply' for lubricated contacts -- cite PhysX docs
)

# 3. Bind the material to BOTH gripper jaws and the casting (physics:material:binding)
apply_physics_material(
    prim_path="<GRIPPER_JAW_LEFT>",
    material_prim="/World/Materials/OiledSteel"
)
apply_physics_material(
    prim_path="<GRIPPER_JAW_RIGHT>",
    material_prim="/World/Materials/OiledSteel"
)
apply_physics_material(
    prim_path="<CASTING_PRIM_PATH>",
    material_prim="/World/Materials/OiledSteel"
)

# 4. Ground the combine-mode semantics
lookup_knowledge(query="PhysX frictionCombineMode average min multiply default Isaac Sim")

# 5. Grasp-and-lift validation -- ablate friction down until slip, confirm threshold
run_usd_script(script="""
# Close gripper, lift for N steps, check casting relative z-velocity > threshold => slip
# Repeat with mu_s in {0.05, 0.10, 0.15, 0.20}; plot slip-vs-mu to confirm threshold
print('ablation results -> JSON for the design review')
""")

# Honest caveat to Kenji:
#  Oil introduces Stribeck (speed-dependent) friction; PhysX is Coulomb only.
#  Sim = drier than real oiled steel. Add an extra safety margin on gripper force.
failure_modes: Leaving frictionCombineMode at default — effective mu is a surprise
Cranking mu to 1.0 to hide slip — masks real gripper-force-margin problems
Binding material only to gripper, not casting — combine mode sees default mu on one side
Ignoring Stribeck — sim passes, real robot slips on oiled surface
